steps: Notice that each of B’s first five values is exactly 1 more than A’s: 9=8+1, 12=11+1, 13=12+1, 16=15+1, 22=21+1. Shifting every value by the same constant does not change the standard deviation. So if \(h = 28\) (= 27+1), then SD(B) = SD(A) exactly — equal, not less.
Compute Variance(A): Mean A = 94/6 ≈ 15.667. Sum of squared deviations ≈ 251.3, so Variance(A) = 251.3/6 ≈ 41.9.

Test \(h=23\): Mean B = 95/6 ≈ 15.833. Sum of squared deviations ≈ 159, Variance(B) ≈ 26.5. Since 26.5 < 41.9, SD(A) > SD(B). ✓
Test \(h=28\): Variance(B) = Variance(A) ≈ 41.9 (equal shift). SD(A) > SD(B) is NOT satisfied. ✗
Test \(h=29\): Mean B = 101/6 ≈ 16.833. Sum of squared deviations ≈ 274.8, Variance(B) ≈ 45.8. Since 45.8 > 41.9, SD(A) < SD(B). ✗

• Choice A — 'Ι only': Only \(h=23\) makes SD(A) > SD(B). ✓
  • Choice B — 'ΙΙ only': \(h=28\) gives SD(A) = SD(B), not strictly greater. ✗
  • Choice C — 'ΙΙΙ only': \(h=29\) gives SD(B) > SD(A), the opposite of the required condition. ✗
  • Choice D — 'ΙΙ or ΙΙΙ': Neither II nor III satisfies the condition. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
